---
title: "Labor OS and Typed Jobs"
chapter_id: "labor-os-and-typed-jobs"
part_id: "planning-memory-reasoning-execution"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "talos"
  - "viea"
  - "genesiscode"
  - "software_magic_grimoire"
  - "talos_md"
  - "moecot"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Labor OS and Typed Jobs

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `labor-os-and-typed-jobs` |
| Part | Part II - Planning, Memory, Reasoning, and Execution |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `talos`, `viea`, `genesiscode`, `software_magic_grimoire`, `talos_md`, `moecot` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

Intelligence must become typed work with lifecycle, permissions, artifacts, logs, and approvals.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Chat outputs do not provide deterministic job lifecycle, tool isolation, auditability, or repeatable cognitive manufacturing.

## Core Claim

[labor-os-and-typed-jobs.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] The execution layer should convert plans into typed jobs managed by a governed labor operating system.

## Mechanism

- Represent jobs with type, inputs, tools, permissions, expected artifacts, approval gates, and failure behavior.
- Separate orchestration, secure workspace/logistics, security/access controls, secret isolation, and audit as explicit execution subfunctions.
- Isolate tools and runtime adapters.
- Record job lifecycle and evidence artifacts.

## Interfaces

- Planning dispatches jobs.
- Security mediates permissions.
- Artifact graph receives outputs.

## Invariants

- Every job has type and lifecycle state.
- Tool permissions are explicit.
- Human approval gates block high-impact jobs.

## Failure modes

- Tool overreach.
- Unlogged side effects.
- Infinite or chaotic agent swarms.

## Minimal implementation

A typed-job schema with lifecycle states and permission checks.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Typed job lifecycle test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Tool permission enforcement test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Human approval gate test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `talos` | Talos Protocol | Planned use from inventory/manifest: AI labor OS. Deterministic cognitive manufacturing, typed jobs, control planes, auditability, tool isolation. |
| `viea` | Verified Intent-to-Execution Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Keystone source. Human intent -> command contracts -> artifacts -> routing -> runtime targets -> verification -> deployment -> feedback. |
| `genesiscode` | GenesisCode | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Tiny pure calculus + obligations + provenance for auditable AI-symbiotic programming. |
| `software_magic_grimoire` | Software Magic Grimoire | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Structured command contracts and semantic density of software words. |
| `talos_md` | Talos_Protocol_v1.0.md | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Markdown/public release version. |
| `moecot` | MoECOT-Agent Architecture Whitepaper | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Concrete implementation evidence: governed low-parameter multi-core runtime, readiness gates, ledgers, replay. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
